annotator_1.text: The framework integrates three mechanisms: (1) Turning Ratio Randomization, a training strategy that exposes agents to dynamic turning probabilities to enhance robustness against unseen scenarios; (2) a stability-oriented Exponential Phase Duration Adjustment action space, which balances responsiveness and precision through cyclical, exponential phase adjustments; and (3) a Neighbor-Based Observation scheme utilizing the MAPPO algorithm with Centralized Training with Decentralized Execution (CTDE).
annotator_1.source_quote: The framework integrates three mechanisms: (1) Turning Ratio Randomization, a training strategy that exposes agents to dynamic turning probabilities to enhance robustness against unseen scenarios; (2) a stability-oriented Exponential Phase Duration Adjustment action space, which balances responsiveness and precision through cyclical, exponential phase adjustments; and (3) a Neighbor-Based Observation scheme utilizing the MAPPO algorithm with Centralized Training with Decentralized Execution (CTDE).

annotator_1.text: Experimental results demonstrate that our framework outperforms standard RL baselines, reducing average waiting time by over 10%.
annotator_1.source_quote: Experimental results demonstrate that our framework outperforms standard RL baselines, reducing average waiting time by over 10%.

annotator_1.text: The proposed model exhibits superior generalization in unseen traffic scenarios and maintains high control stability.
annotator_1.source_quote: The proposed model exhibits superior generalization in unseen traffic scenarios and maintains high control stability, offering a practical solution for adaptive signal control.

annotator_2.text: Reinforcement Learning in Traffic Signal Control faces significant hurdles in real-world deployment due to limited generalization to dynamic traffic flow variations.
annotator_2.source_quote: Reinforcement Learning (RL) in Traffic Signal Control (TSC) faces significant hurdles in real-world deployment due to limited generalization to dynamic traffic flow variations.

annotator_2.text: Existing approaches in Traffic Signal Control often overfit static patterns.
annotator_2.source_quote: Existing approaches often overfit static patterns and use action spaces incompatible with driver expectations.

annotator_2.text: The proposed Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning framework was validated in the Vissim traffic simulator.
annotator_2.source_quote: This paper proposes a robust Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) framework validated in the Vissim traffic simulator.

The framework integrates three mechanisms: (1) Turning Ratio Randomization, a training strategy that exposes agents to dynamic turning probabilities to enhance robustness against unseen scenarios; (2) a stability-oriented Exponential Phase Duration Adjustment action space, which balances responsiveness and precision through cyclical, exponential phase adjustments; and (3) a Neighbor-Based Observation scheme utilizing the MAPPO algorithm with Centralized Training with Decentralized Execution (CTDE).
